learning: Source-hygiene guard tests must match usage patterns (import/require/call), not bare keywords - a doc comment mentioning the banned thing ('no dotenv') tripped /dotenv/.

This machine's npm blocks postinstall scripts (allow-scripts policy). esbuild/vitest still work via optional-dep platform binaries, but if a tool fails with a missing-binary error, check 'npm approve-scripts' first.
Repo is ESM + NodeNext: relative TS imports need .js extensions; new module dirs must be added to tsconfig include.
Connection-string passwords containing + $ / , must be percent-encoded or pg mis-parses the URI. Encode before building DATABASE_URL.
Transactions must run on one dedicated pool connection (pool.connect), never via pool.query - BEGIN and COMMIT can land on different connections.
When applying schema via the Supabase MCP instead of the repo migration applier, seed the repo's schema_migrations ledger inside the same migration so the applier stays idempotent.
Supabase exposes tables via anon-key REST by default; for direct-pg-only apps, enabling RLS with zero policies closes that path without affecting the postgres-role connection.
PumpPortal-style trade-local clients blind-sign server-built transactions. Adapter must verify fee payer + program ids after deserializing, before signing.
A library that reads the key env var internally breaks single-reader key isolation even if its handling is clean. Vendor with keypair injection instead of installing.
Audit untrusted npm code with install --ignore-scripts; check deps' lifecycle scripts explicitly (bigint-buffer/bufferutil/utf-8-validate are normal node-gyp cases).

Parallel agents on disjoint module dirs work cleanly IF the orchestrator pre-claims shared files (tsconfig include, board) and forbids agents from tracking-file edits/commits - agents return handoff content, orchestrator records it.
Trailing stops must arm only above entry price, or a flat position's trail percentage pre-empts the wider hard stop.
